content.claim: Upper grades (5–8) of ordinary primary schools nationally experience 'dilution' (felhígulás) in teaching staff and peer composition as students depart for gimnázium or higher-status schools, creating excess capacity in feeder schools and staffing complications while concentrating demand on selective urban institutions.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Mechanism is widely reported qualitatively but no systematic quantitative audit of affected schools provided
Rural case example involves a single small settlement; generalizability to broader network not established
Does not estimate scale of empty capacity in feeder schools or staffing reductions incurred
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
